rows.notes: Centered page heading. No amounts in the row; heading text legible though a small flourish makes the first word slightly uncertain (interpreted as 'Dno').
Clear reading. Pounds/shillings/pence columns unambiguous; the rightmost numerals read as '19 00 00' with strong alignment to the ruling columns.

rows.description: pro Augm. Red. South Newinton & West.
rows.notes: Description legible. Amount clearly '25 00 00' aligned in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; minor ink blotting at the end of the description but not affecting the numbers.
Text and amount clearly written. Pounds read as '32' with '00 00' in shillings/pence.

rows.description: pro tribus partibus Aug. Red. Rid. Rech.
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings columns align to '19 00'. Pence appears as '10' with a small trailing loop interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny) → 10½d. Slight ink thinning on the fraction symbol, but alignment supports reading.
Description abbreviation (D.B.B.) is somewhat unclear but the monetary columns are well aligned and read '18 06 08'. Chose this reading due to clear digit shapes in the three money columns.

rows.description: pro 4ta parte Aug. Red. Rid. Rech.
rows.notes: Amount reads as '06 12 03' with a small halfpenny loop after the pence digit interpreted as 3½d. Columns are consistently aligned; fraction symbol is faint but present.
Description contains an abbreviation ('Mue.') that is slightly ambiguous, but the monetary figures '24 05 00' are clearly written and aligned.
Description legible; amount clearly '16 08 09' with good column alignment and no visible fraction marks.
Centered sub-heading introducing a list of names below. No amounts on this row.
Single-name entry with a clear amount '01 17 08'. Alignment of the three money columns is strong and digits are distinct.
Name and amount legible; amount reads '03 06 08' with consistent column placement and clear numerals.

Amount looks like '04 15 07' with a small halfpenny loop after the pence digit interpreted as 7½d. The fraction mark is fainter than whole digits but consistent with other halfpenny marks on the page.
Amount appears as '01 00 01'. The pence digit is small but aligns with the pence column; chosen reading over alternate possibility because the pounds column clearly contains '01'.
Pounds and shillings read as '07 16'. Pence looks like '06' with a faint fractional loop — interpreted as 6½d. The fraction is faint making the fraction reading somewhat uncertain.
Clearly marked total on the right with a currency mark and numerals '179 10 02'. Alignment with the pounds/shillings/pence ruling lines is unambiguous.
Centered section header. No monetary amounts on the header row; heading script is clear.

rows.description: Nov. 4 To mr Wood resid. of South Newin Stip due to yre year there Mic. 1743. & unpd at the last Audit day
rows.notes: Date and long description legible though cramped. Amount '02 02 06' aligns to the money columns clearly; wording irregularities preserved as in the original.
Date '9' and description clear. Amount reads '05 11 06' with clean digits and correct column alignment.
Amount appears as '00 06 00' (6s 0d). The description has a small ink flourish but the numeric columns are clear.

Pounds read as '33' which is larger than most nearby items; digits are legible but unusually large value makes it slightly suspect. Alignment is consistent with pounds column and shillings/pence read as '08 00'.
Small sub-line under the Wilmot payment. Amount reads '00 07 06'. The pence digits are fairly clear; this appears to be a related sub-item (wine) and aligns to the money columns.

Description 'Mrs Jaques' is somewhat ambiguous (handwriting), and the amount is less clear than some lines; read as '05 01 04' from alignment of digits. Slight ink bleed on the pence makes exact pence digit uncertain but 4d fits the visible strokes.

rows.description: 30 to Coffee on y Audit day
rows.notes: Short entry and amount '00 02 08' are clear; the words 'Coffee on y Audit day' are legible and the money columns well aligned.
Amount reads '00 11 00' clearly. Name 'Mr Franklin' legible; small ink smudge does not affect the numbers.
The left-side date/notation ('9 2') is compact and a little ambiguous; amount reads '00 12 06' (12s 6d). Chose this over other placements because digits line up clearly in the money columns.

rows.description: 2 to Mr Brooks for bringing Tablone Rent
rows.notes: Date '2' and description legible though cramped. Monetary columns read '00 02 06'. The phrase 'Tablone Rent' is faint but plausible; amount digits are unambiguous.

rows.description: 12 pd Newmans Land tax to day 1743
rows.notes: Amount reads '00 18 08' clearly and aligns with the date '12' and the description 'Newmans Land tax'. Handwriting in description is slightly compressed but legible.
Short entry; amount '00 03 00' is clear. The last word of description reads 'Junins' (binding of something) — legible though archaic spelling.

rows.description: 24 to ys Cook his years Act & of Act Dinner
rows.notes: Description is somewhat abbreviated ('ys Cook', 'years Act'); monetary amount reads '04 14 04' with clear digits. The wording is preserved as in the original line.

rows.description: to Mr Hillow per for Recover? Rundle's Battley
rows.notes: Description contains several unclear names/words ('Hillow', 'Rundles Battley') — these are transcribed as they appear. Amount reads '00 13 04' but both a slightly faded ink and unusual wording reduce confidence.
